data.title: Trump leans toward picking Hassett or Warsh for next Fed chair - report
data.description:
National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett is widely seen as the top candidate to run the Federal Reserve, although U.S. President Donald Trump signaled that former Fed governor Kevin Warsh is also still very much in the running.

In an interview with the Wall Street Journal, Trump said "I think the two Kevins are great." I think there are a couple of other people that are great," he said in the Oval Office

Warsh's chances of succeeding current Fed chief Jerome Powell next year jumped after the news to 36% from around 20%, according to prediction marketplace Kalshi (KALSHI). Conversely, Hassett's odds fell to 60% from around 70%.

Trump questioned Warsh during an interview earlier this week about his willingness to back interest-rate cuts if selected as Fed chair, the WSJ reported, citing people familiar. Warsh thinks lower rates are necessary, he added, "and so does everybody else that I've talked to." Trump recently said that lowering rates would be a litmus test in the Fed chair selection process.

The president told the Journal that he wants rates to land at "1% and maybe lower than that" a year from now. The Fed's policy rate target range stands at 3.50%-3.75%, a three-year low, after another quarter-point cut this past week.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: IM Cannabis appoints Asi Levi as CFO
data.description:
• IM Cannabis (NASDAQ:IMCC) announced on Friday the appointment of Asi Levi, CPA, as the company's CFO, effective immediately, succeeding Uri Birenberg.
  • Levi brings over 15 years of experience in finance and controlling roles. He served as CFO of Water Ways Technologies and DekelOil and as controller of SciSparc and A2Z Cust2Mate Solutions.
  • Shares +12.57%.

data.title: US said to have renewed license for Chevron to pump oil in Venezuela - report
data.description:
The Trump administration on Thursday reportedly renewed a license for oil major Chevron (CVX) to continue pumping oil in Venezuela.

According to a Bloomberg report, citing sources familiar with the matter, the license allows Chevron to operate in four joint ventures with Venezuela’s national oil company Petróleos de Venezuela SA as an exception to longstanding oil sanctions on the country.

This renewal came a day after U.S. forces seized a sanctioned oil tanker off the coast of Venezuela amid rising tension between Washington and Caracas.

Houston, Texas-based Chevron and The U.S. Treasury Department did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

The Trump administration has barred most U.S. companies from doing business in Venezuela, but it allows Chevron to maintain minimal maintenance on equipment in its joint ventures and to keep exporting Venezuelan oil to the U.S.

Earlier in July, Bloomberg reported that Chevron would hand over a percentage of its Venezuelan production to the Maduro government under new terms negotiated after receiving a U.S. license to restart operations.

data.title: Alector appoints Neil Berkley as chief financial officer
data.description:
• Alector (ALEC) said on Friday that Neil Berkley has been appointed CFO, effective December 10, 2025.
  • Berkley has served as Alector’s Chief Business Officer (CBO) since March 2024, and CBO and Interim Chief Financial Officer (CFO) since June 2025. He will continue to serve as CBO.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: System1 receives notice from NYSE
data.description:
• System1 (NYSE:SST) received on Friday notice from the NYSE that it is currently not in compliance with Section 802.01B of the NYSE Listed Company Manual because its 30-trading-day average market capitalization and its last reported stockholder’s equity as of September 30, 2025, were less than $50M.
  • The company will notify the NYSE by December 22, 2025, that it intends to submit a business plan by January 22, 2026, to meet the market capitalization to comply with the NYSE's continued listing standards.

data.title: Southwest opens new crew base in Austin; extends winning streak to 9 straight days
data.description:
Southwest Airlines (LUV) is opening a new crew base for pilots and flight attendants at the Austin Bergstrom International Airport in Texas, providing an operational base for employees and expanding the carrier’s presence in Central Texas.

Southwest (LUV) is currently the largest carrier at the airport, serving more than 6.7 million passengers last year. Southwest (LUV) will begin new service from Bergstrom to popular destinations like Fort Myers, Florida, Palm Springs, California, and Steamboat Springs, Colorado beginning this spring coinciding with the opening of the new crew base.

The new facility is expected to create more than 2,000 new jobs.

Under the direction of CEO Bob Jordan, Southwest (LUV) is in the midst of a strategic overhaul to create new revenue streams and change its long-standing boarding process.

The changes follow heightened pressure from activist investor Elliott Management, which holds nearly 15% of Southwest’s (LUV) shares and is pushing for stronger operational performance, a more independent board, and leadership changes.

To appease Elliott and avoid a proxy fight, Southwest (LUV) restructured its board by “fast-tracking” the retirement of former CEO and executive chairman Gary Kelly and 6 directors on the board and replacing them with five Elliott-nominated candidates.

With Elliott pacified and the airline’s cost-cutting and revenue efforts paying off, the stock has gained altitude, up 42% from the November low of $29.67. Shares closed higher again on Friday, extending its winning streak to a ninth day, and closing Friday’s session at a 3 ½ year high.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P 500 end week with losses as AI trade tumbles led by Broadcom guidance fears
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages ended lower on Friday, with the S&P 500 ((SP500)) and the Nasdaq ((COMP:IND)) ending the week in the red as the AI trade came under pressure with Broadcom ((AVGO)) leading the decline.

The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) closed -1.7%, while the S&P 500 (SP500) finished -1.1%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI), which had earlier reached new highs, retreated to end -0.5%.

Week-to-date, the S&P 500 ((SP500)) lost -0.6%, and the Nasdaq ((COMP:IND) shed -1.6%, while the Dow ((DJI)) gained +1%.

Chipmaker Broadcom (AVGO) -11.6% warned of margin pressures and disappointed investors with a lesser-than-expected backlog figure for AI product orders.

“This week the market had a lot to think about. Earnings from Broadcom ((AVGO)), perhaps the key bellwether AI name now, and the small matter of the price of money and the supply thereof, courtesy of Chairman Powell,” Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research said.

“FOMC was plain bullish in our view – cheaper money and more liquidity; and with AVGO printing sustained high growth, high margins and bullish outlook, we have no reason to be bearish into year-end, panicans notwithstanding. Higher,” King added.

Also, among the Nasdaq’s ((COMP:IND)) top decliners are Micron Technology (MU), Applovin (APP), Palantir (PLTR), and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), ending -6.7%, -6.5%, -2.2%, and -4.9%, respectively.

On the economic side, Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee said that waiting on more data before lowering interest rates would have been more prudent. Also, Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack said she would prefer a “slightly more” restrictive monetary policy.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.20%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was 1 basis point lower to 3.54%.

data.title: Corning slides after six-session rally
data.description:
Corning Incorporated (GLW) shares fell 7.96% to close at $88.83 on Friday, ending a six-session winning streak amid broader market softness.

The stock had surged about 12.3% between December 4 and December 11, far outpacing the S&P 500’s 0.64% rise over the same period.

The company last reported in late October, posting better-than-expected Q3 results with sales and earnings rising solidly. Executives said demand for its GenAI and solar products remained strong and projected another quarter of growth, while also expecting to reach a 20% operating margin earlier than planned.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, Corning is rated with a Strong Buy, with grades of A in momentum and revisions, B+ in terms of both growth and profitability, while a D in valuation.

However, SA analysts suggest holding the stock.

Analyst Daniel Jones said Corning is showing strong momentum in its core businesses and benefiting from AI and solar demand, the stock’s valuation remains a concern. He added that “shares remain expensive relative to peers, presenting valuation risks for value-oriented investors.”

Turning to the Wall Street, analysts are largely bullish, with 11 out of 14 analysts rating the stock with a Buy or higher, two rate it with a Hold and only one of them suggest a strong sell.

The stock remains up 98.21% year-to-date and has inched 0.47% higher over the past month.

data.title: Tether proposes to buy Exor's majority stake in Juventus Football Club
data.description:
Tether, the company behind USDT (USDT-USD), the world's largest stablecoin, has proposed to buy Exor's entire stake in Juventus Football Club (JVTSF) in a binding all-cash deal, it said on Friday.

Tether already owns a portion of Juventus (JVTSF), having boosted its stake in April to over 10.1%. Exor's shareholding represents 65.4% of Juventus’ (JVTSF) issued share capital. 

Upon completion of the planned deal, Tether intends to proceed with a public tender offer for the remaining shares at the same price per share, fully funded with its own capital, it said in a statement.

Should the transaction be completed, Tether said it will invest 1B euros ($1.17B) "in the support and development of the club."

data.title: Kevin Warsh 'would make a great' Fed chief, JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon says
data.description:
Former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh "would make a great" leader of the U.S. central bank, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon said during a recent event, according to a media report on Friday.

Warsh was previously considered for Fed chair during President Donald Trump's first term.

National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett, who is still widely seen as the top candidate to succeed current Fed chief Jerome Powell in May 2026, would most likely reduce interest rates in the near term, aligning with Trump's view, Dimon said at the private event, Barron's reported. 

The NEC head urged the Fed last week to keep lowering rates, correctly predicting a third consecutive quarter-point reduction at this week's policy meeting.
